== History == Originally, the shrine was located atop Tsurugi Rock (剱岩), referred to as "Biwanku" (琵琶甲 or 枇琶甲), with a stone sanctuary at the summit and a worship hall at the base. In December 1674 (Enpō 1), it was relocated to its current site. The name "Tsurugi" derives from a legend where Yamato Takeru climbed the rock while scouting the {{ill|Kumaso|ja|熊襲|qq=draft|qq=draft}} during his conquest, resting with his sword.
During the reign of the Shikine clan, the shrine fell into neglect as they venerated {{ill|Tenmangu|ja|天満宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}} as their ancestral shrine. It was later restored by {{ill|Shimazu Yoshihisa|ja|島津義久|qq=draft|qq=draft}} under the order of {{ill|Yamada Toshiyasu|ja|山田利安|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Records of this restoration exist in the "Shrine Registry" and "Tsurugi Shrine History" preserved by the Saruwatari family of Shikine.
On October 25, 1910, the shrine amalgamated with Hyōsu Shrine, Sugawara Shrine, Futodama Shrine, and Hiruko Shrine.
== Festivals and Rituals == The annual "Shinkōsai" (神幸祭) or "Mikoshi Procession" originates from a historical event where a summer epidemic was halted after a purification ritual performed by the shrine. The festival is now held to pray for health and protection against epidemics.
Additionally, monthly rituals are conducted on the 1st and 15th at 6 AM.
== Amalgamated Shrines == ;Hyōsu Shrine : Deity and origin unknown. Previously located in Hamaji Setta, Shikine Village, with a tiled main hall and a dance hall.
;Sugawara Shrine : Dedicated to {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Originally located in Setta, Shikine Village. Historical records mention its reconstruction in 1602 (Keichō 7) by Lord Yoshihisa Shimazu.
;Futodama Shrine : Dedicated to {{ill|Futodama|ja|太玉命|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Details unknown. Originally had a tiled main hall, dance hall, and bridge hall.
;Hiruko Shrine : Dedicated to {{ill|Hiruko|ja|蛭子命|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Once featured a stone sanctuary in Setta, Shikine Village.
== Gallery == * Tsurugi Rock within the shrine precincts * Site of Futodama Shrine, now Shikine Community Hall * Site of Hiruko Shrine with a modern concrete sanctuary * Statue of Ebisu at the former site of Hiruko Shrine
== References == * "Furusato Shikine no Kamigami" edited by Tsurugi Shrine Chief Priest Ikeda Shin

'''Tsurugi Shrine''' (剱神社) often written as "剣神社," is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} located in {{ill|Kokubu Shikine|ja|国分敷根|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Kirishima, Kagoshima|ja|霧島市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Kagoshima Prefecture|ja|鹿児島県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.
== Deities Enshrined == The principal deity is {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武命|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Additionally, the shrine enshrines {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane|ja|天児屋根命|1=Ame-no-Koyane|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, "Tsurugihiko no Kami" (剱彦神), {{ill|Oōusunomiko|ja|大碓皇子|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, "Kosunomiko" (小臼命, identified with Yamato Takeru), {{ill|Futodama|ja|太玉命|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. These deities were enshrined through amalgamation in 1910 (Meiji 43).
== History == Originally, the shrine was located atop Tsurugi Rock (剱岩), referred to as "Biwanku" (琵琶甲 or 枇琶甲), with a stone sanctuary at the summit and a worship hall at the base. In December 1674 (Enpō 1), it was relocated to its current site. The name "Tsurugi" derives from a legend where Yamato Takeru climbed the rock while scouting the {{ill|Kumaso|ja|熊襲|qq=draft|qq=draft}} during his conquest, resting with his sword.
During the reign of the Shikine clan, the shrine fell into neglect as they venerated {{ill|Tenmangu|ja|天満宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}} as their ancestral shrine. It was later restored by {{ill|Shimazu Yoshihisa|ja|島津義久|qq=draft|qq=draft}} under the order of {{ill|Yamada Toshiyasu|ja|山田利安|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Records of this restoration exist in the "Shrine Registry" and "Tsurugi Shrine History" preserved by the Saruwatari family of Shikine.
On October 25, 1910, the shrine amalgamated with Hyōsu Shrine, Sugawara Shrine, Futodama Shrine, and Hiruko Shrine.
== Festivals and Rituals == The annual "Shinkōsai" (神幸祭) or "Mikoshi Procession" originates from a historical event where a summer epidemic was halted after a purification ritual performed by the shrine. The festival is now held to pray for health and protection against epidemics.
Additionally, monthly rituals are conducted on the 1st and 15th at 6 AM.
== Amalgamated Shrines == ;Hyōsu Shrine : Deity and origin unknown. Previously located in Hamaji Setta, Shikine Village, with a tiled main hall and a dance hall.
;Sugawara Shrine : Dedicated to {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Originally located in Setta, Shikine Village. Historical records mention its reconstruction in 1602 (Keichō 7) by Lord Yoshihisa Shimazu.
;Futodama Shrine : Dedicated to {{ill|Futodama|ja|太玉命|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Details unknown. Originally had a tiled main hall, dance hall, and bridge hall.
;Hiruko Shrine : Dedicated to {{ill|Hiruko|ja|蛭子命|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Once featured a stone sanctuary in Setta, Shikine Village.
== Gallery == * Tsurugi Rock within the shrine precincts * Site of Futodama Shrine, now Shikine Community Hall * Site of Hiruko Shrine with a modern concrete sanctuary * Statue of Ebisu at the former site of Hiruko Shrine
== References == * "Furusato Shikine no Kamigami" edited by Tsurugi Shrine Chief Priest Ikeda Shin
== External Links == * [http://www.kagojinjacho.or.jp/search/airaisa/kirishima/post-687.html Tsurugi Shrine (Kagoshima Shrine Association)] {{citation needed|Tsurugi Shrine (Kirishima)}}
{{DEFAULTSORT:Tsurugi Shrine (Kirishima)}} {{translated page|ja|剱神社|version=91787801|comment=Imported full ja history}}

revision.comment: Copying from [[Category:Pages that were shrank]] to [[Category:Pages likely having an accidental overwite in their history that shrank them]] using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
'''Tsurugi Shrine''' (剱神社) often written as "剣神社," is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} located in {{ill|Kokubu Shikine|ja|国分敷根|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Kirishima, Kagoshima|ja|霧島市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Kagoshima Prefecture|ja|鹿児島県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.
== Deities Enshrined == The principal deity is {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武命|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Additionally, the shrine enshrines {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane|ja|天児屋根命|1=Ame-no-Koyane|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, "Tsurugihiko no Kami" (剱彦神), {{ill|Oōusunomiko|ja|大碓皇子|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, "Kosunomiko" (小臼命, identified with Yamato Takeru), {{ill|Futodama|ja|太玉命|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. These deities were enshrined through amalgamation in 1910 (Meiji 43).
== History == Originally, the shrine was located atop Tsurugi Rock (剱岩), referred to as "Biwanku" (琵琶甲 or 枇琶甲), with a stone sanctuary at the summit and a worship hall at the base. In December 1674 (Enpō 1), it was relocated to its current site. The name "Tsurugi" derives from a legend where Yamato Takeru climbed the rock while scouting the {{ill|Kumaso|ja|熊襲|qq=draft|qq=draft}} during his conquest, resting with his sword.
During the reign of the Shikine clan, the shrine fell into neglect as they venerated {{ill|Tenmangu|ja|天満宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}} as their ancestral shrine. It was later restored by {{ill|Shimazu Yoshihisa|ja|島津義久|qq=draft|qq=draft}} under the order of {{ill|Yamada Toshiyasu|ja|山田利安|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Records of this restoration exist in the "Shrine Registry" and "Tsurugi Shrine History" preserved by the Saruwatari family of Shikine.
On October 25, 1910, the shrine amalgamated with Hyōsu Shrine, Sugawara Shrine, Futodama Shrine, and Hiruko Shrine.
== Festivals and Rituals == The annual "Shinkōsai" (神幸祭) or "Mikoshi Procession" originates from a historical event where a summer epidemic was halted after a purification ritual performed by the shrine. The festival is now held to pray for health and protection against epidemics.
Additionally, monthly rituals are conducted on the 1st and 15th at 6 AM.
== Amalgamated Shrines == ;Hyōsu Shrine : Deity and origin unknown. Previously located in Hamaji Setta, Shikine Village, with a tiled main hall and a dance hall.
;Sugawara Shrine : Dedicated to {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Originally located in Setta, Shikine Village. Historical records mention its reconstruction in 1602 (Keichō 7) by Lord Yoshihisa Shimazu.
;Futodama Shrine : Dedicated to {{ill|Futodama|ja|太玉命|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Details unknown. Originally had a tiled main hall, dance hall, and bridge hall.
;Hiruko Shrine : Dedicated to {{ill|Hiruko|ja|蛭子命|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Once featured a stone sanctuary in Setta, Shikine Village.
== Gallery == * Tsurugi Rock within the shrine precincts * Site of Futodama Shrine, now Shikine Community Hall * Site of Hiruko Shrine with a modern concrete sanctuary * Statue of Ebisu at the former site of Hiruko Shrine
== References == * "Furusato Shikine no Kamigami" edited by Tsurugi Shrine Chief Priest Ikeda Shin
== External Links == * [http://www.kagojinjacho.or.jp/search/airaisa/kirishima/post-687.html Tsurugi Shrine (Kagoshima Shrine Association)] {{citation needed|Tsurugi Shrine (Kirishima)}}
{{DEFAULTSORT:Tsurugi Shrine (Kirishima)}} {{translated page|ja|剱神社|version=91787801|comment=Imported full ja history}}

Additionally, monthly rituals are conducted on the 1st and 15th at 6 AM.

revision.text:
'''Tsurugi Shrine''' (剱神社) often written as "剣神社," is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} located in {{ill|Kokubu Shikine|ja|国分敷根|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Kirishima, Kagoshima|ja|霧島市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Kagoshima Prefecture|ja|鹿児島県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.
== Deities Enshrined == The principal deity is {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武命|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Additionally, the shrine enshrines {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane|ja|天児屋根命|1=Ame-no-Koyane|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, "Tsurugihiko no Kami" (剱彦神), {{ill|Oōusunomiko|ja|大碓皇子|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, "Kosunomiko" (小臼命, identified with Yamato Takeru), {{ill|Futodama|ja|太玉命|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. These deities were enshrined through amalgamation in 1910 (Meiji 43).
== History == Originally, the shrine was located atop Tsurugi Rock (剱岩), referred to as "Biwanku" (琵琶甲 or 枇琶甲), with a stone sanctuary at the summit and a worship hall at the base. In December 1674 (Enpō 1), it was relocated to its current site. The name "Tsurugi" derives from a legend where Yamato Takeru climbed the rock while scouting the {{ill|Kumaso|ja|熊襲|qq=draft|qq=draft}} during his conquest, resting with his sword.
During the reign of the Shikine clan, the shrine fell into neglect as they venerated {{ill|Tenmangu|ja|天満宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}} as their ancestral shrine. It was later restored by {{ill|Shimazu Yoshihisa|ja|島津義久|qq=draft|qq=draft}} under the order of {{ill|Yamada Toshiyasu|ja|山田利安|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Records of this restoration exist in the "Shrine Registry" and "Tsurugi Shrine History" preserved by the Saruwatari family of Shikine.
On October 25, 1910, the shrine amalgamated with Hyōsu Shrine, Sugawara Shrine, Futodama Shrine, and Hiruko Shrine.
== Festivals and Rituals == The annual "Shinkōsai" (神幸祭) or "Mikoshi Procession" originates from a historical event where a summer epidemic was halted after a purification ritual performed by the shrine. The festival is now held to pray for health and protection against epidemics.
Additionally, monthly rituals are conducted on the 1st and 15th at 6 AM.
== Amalgamated Shrines == ;Hyōsu Shrine : Deity and origin unknown. Previously located in Hamaji Setta, Shikine Village, with a tiled main hall and a dance hall.
;Sugawara Shrine : Dedicated to {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Originally located in Setta, Shikine Village. Historical records mention its reconstruction in 1602 (Keichō 7) by Lord Yoshihisa Shimazu.
;Futodama Shrine : Dedicated to {{ill|Futodama|ja|太玉命|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Details unknown. Originally had a tiled main hall, dance hall, and bridge hall.
;Hiruko Shrine : Dedicated to {{ill|Hiruko|ja|蛭子命|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Once featured a stone sanctuary in Setta, Shikine Village.
== Gallery == * Tsurugi Rock within the shrine precincts * Site of Futodama Shrine, now Shikine Community Hall * Site of Hiruko Shrine with a modern concrete sanctuary * Statue of Ebisu at the former site of Hiruko Shrine
== References == * "Furusato Shikine no Kamigami" edited by Tsurugi Shrine Chief Priest Ikeda Shin
== External Links == * [http://www.kagojinjacho.or.jp/search/airaisa/kirishima/post-687.html Tsurugi Shrine (Kagoshima Shrine Association)] {{citation needed|Tsurugi Shrine (Kirishima)}}

These deities were enshrined through amalgamation in 1910 (Meiji 43).

Records of this restoration exist in the "Shrine Registry" and "Tsurugi Shrine History" preserved by the Saruwatari family of Shikine.

'''Tsurugi Shrine''' (剱神社) often written as "剣神社," is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} located in {{ill|Kokubu Shikine|ja|国分敷根|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Kirishima, Kagoshima|ja|霧島市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Kagoshima Prefecture|ja|鹿児島県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.
== Deities Enshrined == The principal deity is {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武命|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Additionally, the shrine enshrines {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane|ja|天児屋根命|1=Ame-no-Koyane|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, "Tsurugihiko no Kami" (剱彦神), {{ill|Oōusunomiko|ja|大碓皇子|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, "Kosunomiko" (小臼命, identified with Yamato Takeru), {{ill|Futodama|ja|太玉命|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. These deities were enshrined through amalgamation in 1910 (Meiji 43).
== History == Originally, the shrine was located atop Tsurugi Rock (剱岩), referred to as "Biwanku" (琵琶甲 or 枇琶甲), with a stone sanctuary at the summit and a worship hall at the base. In December 1674 (Enpō 1), it was relocated to its current site. The name "Tsurugi" derives from a legend where Yamato Takeru climbed the rock while scouting the {{ill|Kumaso|ja|熊襲|qq=draft|qq=draft}} during his conquest, resting with his sword.
During the reign of the Shikine clan, the shrine fell into neglect as they venerated {{ill|Tenmangu|ja|天満宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}} as their ancestral shrine. It was later restored by {{ill|Shimazu Yoshihisa|ja|島津義久|qq=draft|qq=draft}} under the order of {{ill|Yamada Toshiyasu|ja|山田利安|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Records of this restoration exist in the "Shrine Registry" and "Tsurugi Shrine History" preserved by the Saruwatari family of Shikine.
On October 25, 1910, the shrine amalgamated with Hyōsu Shrine, Sugawara Shrine, Futodama Shrine, and Hiruko Shrine.
== Festivals and Rituals == The annual "Shinkōsai" (神幸祭) or "Mikoshi Procession" originates from a historical event where a summer epidemic was halted after a purification ritual performed by the shrine. The festival is now held to pray for health and protection against epidemics.
Additionally, monthly rituals are conducted on the 1st and 15th at 6 AM.
== Amalgamated Shrines == ;Hyōsu Shrine : Deity and origin unknown. Previously located in Hamaji Setta, Shikine Village, with a tiled main hall and a dance hall.
;Sugawara Shrine : Dedicated to {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Originally located in Setta, Shikine Village. Historical records mention its reconstruction in 1602 (Keichō 7) by Lord Yoshihisa Shimazu.
;Futodama Shrine : Dedicated to {{ill|Futodama|ja|太玉命|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Details unknown. Originally had a tiled main hall, dance hall, and bridge hall.
;Hiruko Shrine : Dedicated to {{ill|Hiruko|ja|蛭子命|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Once featured a stone sanctuary in Setta, Shikine Village.
== Gallery == * Tsurugi Rock within the shrine precincts * Site of Futodama Shrine, now Shikine Community Hall * Site of Hiruko Shrine with a modern concrete sanctuary * Statue of Ebisu at the former site of Hiruko Shrine
== References == * "Furusato Shikine no Kamigami" edited by Tsurugi Shrine Chief Priest Ikeda Shin
== External Links == * [http://www.kagojinjacho.or.jp/search/airaisa/kirishima/post-687.html Tsurugi Shrine (Kagoshima Shrine Association)] {{citation needed|Tsurugi Shrine (Kirishima)}}

Records of this restoration exist in the "Shrine Registry" and "Tsurugi Shrine History" preserved by the Saruwatari family of Shikine.
On October 25, 1910, the shrine amalgamated with Hyōsu Shrine, Sugawara Shrine, Futodama Shrine, and Hiruko Shrine.
== Festivals and Rituals == The annual "Shinkōsai" (神幸祭) or "Mikoshi Procession" originates from a historical event where a summer epidemic was halted after a purification ritual performed by the shrine. The festival is now held to pray for health and protection against epidemics.
Additionally, monthly rituals are conducted on the 1st and 15th at 6 AM.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Tsurugi-jinja (Kirishima)]][[ja:剱神社]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Tsurugi-jinja (Kirishima)]][[ja:剱神社]] '''Tsurugi Shrine''' (剱神社) often written as "剣神社," is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} located in {{ill|Kokubu Shikine|ja|国分敷根|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Kirishima, Kagoshima|ja|霧島市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Kagoshima Prefecture|ja|鹿児島県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.
== Deities Enshrined == The principal deity is {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武命|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Additionally, the shrine enshrines {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane|ja|天児屋根命|1=Ame-no-Koyane|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, "Tsurugihiko no Kami" (剱彦神), {{ill|Oōusunomiko|ja|大碓皇子|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, "Kosunomiko" (小臼命, identified with Yamato Takeru), {{ill|Futodama|ja|太玉命|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. These deities were enshrined through amalgamation in 1910 (Meiji 43).
== History == Originally, the shrine was located atop Tsurugi Rock (剱岩), referred to as "Biwanku" (琵琶甲 or 枇琶甲), with a stone sanctuary at the summit and a worship hall at the base. In December 1674 (Enpō 1), it was relocated to its current site. The name "Tsurugi" derives from a legend where Yamato Takeru climbed the rock while scouting the {{ill|Kumaso|ja|熊襲|qq=draft|qq=draft}} during his conquest, resting with his sword.
During the reign of the Shikine clan, the shrine fell into neglect as they venerated {{ill|Tenmangu|ja|天満宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}} as their ancestral shrine. It was later restored by {{ill|Shimazu Yoshihisa|ja|島津義久|qq=draft|qq=draft}} under the order of {{ill|Yamada Toshiyasu|ja|山田利安|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Records of this restoration exist in the "Shrine Registry" and "Tsurugi Shrine History" preserved by the Saruwatari family of Shikine.
On October 25, 1910, the shrine amalgamated with Hyōsu Shrine, Sugawara Shrine, Futodama Shrine, and Hiruko Shrine.
== Festivals and Rituals == The annual "Shinkōsai" (神幸祭) or "Mikoshi Procession" originates from a historical event where a summer epidemic was halted after a purification ritual performed by the shrine. The festival is now held to pray for health and protection against epidemics.
Additionally, monthly rituals are conducted on the 1st and 15th at 6 AM.
== Amalgamated Shrines == ;Hyōsu Shrine : Deity and origin unknown. Previously located in Hamaji Setta, Shikine Village, with a tiled main hall and a dance hall.
;Sugawara Shrine : Dedicated to {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Originally located in Setta, Shikine Village. Historical records mention its reconstruction in 1602 (Keichō 7) by Lord Yoshihisa Shimazu.
;Futodama Shrine : Dedicated to {{ill|Futodama|ja|太玉命|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Details unknown. Originally had a tiled main hall, dance hall, and bridge hall.
;Hiruko Shrine : Dedicated to {{ill|Hiruko|ja|蛭子命|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Once featured a stone sanctuary in Setta, Shikine Village.
== Gallery == * Tsurugi Rock within the shrine precincts * Site of Futodama Shrine, now Shikine Community Hall * Site of Hiruko Shrine with a modern concrete sanctuary * Statue of Ebisu at the former site of Hiruko Shrine

Additionally, the shrine enshrines {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane|ja|天児屋根命|1=Ame-no-Koyane|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天児屋命}}, "Tsurugihiko no Kami" (剱彦神), {{ill|Oōusunomiko|ja|大碓皇子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oōusunomiko|1=Prince Oousu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11438297}}, "Kosunomiko" (小臼命, identified with Yamato Takeru), {{ill|Futodama|ja|太玉命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futodama|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to フトダマ}}, and {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}}. These deities were enshrined through amalgamation in 1910 (Meiji 43).
== History == Originally, the shrine was located atop Tsurugi Rock (剱岩), referred to as "Biwanku" (琵琶甲 or 枇琶甲), with a stone sanctuary at the summit and a worship hall at the base. In December 1674 (Enpō 1), it was relocated to its current site. The name "Tsurugi" derives from a legend where Yamato Takeru climbed the rock while scouting the [[:en:Kumaso|Kumaso]] during his conquest, resting with his sword.
During the reign of the Shikine clan, the shrine fell into neglect as they venerated [[:en:Tenmangū|Tenmangu]] as their ancestral shrine. It was later restored by [[:en:Shimazu Yoshihisa|Shimazu Yoshihisa]] under the order of {{ill|Yamada Toshiyasu|ja|山田利安|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamada Toshiyasu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Records of this restoration exist in the "Shrine Registry" and "Tsurugi Shrine History" preserved by the Saruwatari family of Shikine.
On October 25, 1910, the shrine amalgamated with Hyōsu Shrine, Sugawara Shrine, Futodama Shrine, and Hiruko Shrine.
== Festivals and Rituals == The annual "Shinkōsai" (神幸祭) or "Mikoshi Procession" originates from a historical event where a summer epidemic was halted after a purification ritual performed by the shrine. The festival is now held to pray for health and protection against epidemics.
Additionally, monthly rituals are conducted on the 1st and 15th at 6 AM.
== Amalgamated Shrines == ;Hyōsu Shrine : Deity and origin unknown. Previously located in Hamaji Setta, Shikine Village, with a tiled main hall and a dance hall.
;Sugawara Shrine : Dedicated to {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}}. Originally located in Setta, Shikine Village. Historical records mention its reconstruction in 1602 (Keichō 7) by Lord Yoshihisa Shimazu.
;Futodama Shrine : Dedicated to {{ill|Futodama|ja|太玉命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futodama|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to フトダマ}}. Details unknown. Originally had a tiled main hall, dance hall, and bridge hall.
;Hiruko Shrine : Dedicated to {{ill|Hiruko|ja|蛭子命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiruko|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヒルコ|1=Hiruko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1067028}}. Once featured a stone sanctuary in Setta, Shikine Village.
== Gallery == * Tsurugi Rock within the shrine precincts * Site of Futodama Shrine, now Shikine Community Hall * Site of Hiruko Shrine with a modern concrete sanctuary * Statue of Ebisu at the former site of Hiruko Shrine
== References == * "Furusato Shikine no Kamigami" edited by Tsurugi Shrine Chief Priest Ikeda Shin
== External Links == * [http://www.kagojinjacho.or.jp/search/airaisa/kirishima/post-687.html Tsurugi Shrine (Kagoshima Shrine Association)]

== History == Originally, the shrine was located atop Tsurugi Rock (剱岩), referred to as "Biwanku" (琵琶甲 or 枇琶甲), with a stone sanctuary at the summit and a worship hall at the base. In December 1674 (Enpō 1), it was relocated to its current site. The name "Tsurugi" derives from a legend where Yamato Takeru climbed the rock while scouting the [[:en:Kumaso|Kumaso]] during his conquest, resting with his sword.
During the reign of the Shikine clan, the shrine fell into neglect as they venerated [[:en:Tenmangū|Tenmangu]] as their ancestral shrine. It was later restored by [[:en:Shimazu Yoshihisa|Shimazu Yoshihisa]] under the order of {{ill|Yamada Toshiyasu|ja|山田利安|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamada Toshiyasu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Records of this restoration exist in the "Shrine Registry" and "Tsurugi Shrine History" preserved by the Saruwatari family of Shikine.
On October 25, 1910, the shrine amalgamated with Hyōsu Shrine, Sugawara Shrine, Futodama Shrine, and Hiruko Shrine.
== Festivals and Rituals == The annual "Shinkōsai" (神幸祭) or "Mikoshi Procession" originates from a historical event where a summer epidemic was halted after a purification ritual performed by the shrine. The festival is now held to pray for health and protection against epidemics.
Additionally, monthly rituals are conducted on the 1st and 15th at 6 AM.
== Amalgamated Shrines == ;Hyōsu Shrine : Deity and origin unknown. Previously located in Hamaji Setta, Shikine Village, with a tiled main hall and a dance hall.
;Sugawara Shrine : Dedicated to {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}}. Originally located in Setta, Shikine Village. Historical records mention its reconstruction in 1602 (Keichō 7) by Lord Yoshihisa Shimazu.
;Futodama Shrine : Dedicated to {{ill|Futodama|ja|太玉命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futodama|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to フトダマ}}. Details unknown. Originally had a tiled main hall, dance hall, and bridge hall.
;Hiruko Shrine : Dedicated to {{ill|Hiruko|ja|蛭子命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiruko|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヒルコ|1=Hiruko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1067028}}. Once featured a stone sanctuary in Setta, Shikine Village.

== Accidentally Overwritten Content ==
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Tsurugi-jinja (Kirishima)]][[ja:剱神社]] {{Infobox religious building|image=File:Shikine-turugi.JPG|prefecture={{ill|Kokubu-Shikine|WD=Q11420538}}|coordinates={{Coord|31.70575|N|130.786528|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|architecture_style={{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|WD=Q930545}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11397991]] Wikidata name: Tsurugi-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:31, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:09, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Tsurugi Shrine''' (剱神社) often written as "剣神社," is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in {{ill|Kokubu Shikine|ja|国分敷根|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokubu Shikine|1=Kokubu-Shikine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11420538}}, [[:en:Kirishima, Kagoshima|Kirishima, Kagoshima]], [[:en:Kagoshima Prefecture|Kagoshima Prefecture]].
== Deities Enshrined == The principal deity is [[:en:Yamato Takeru|Yamato Takeru]]. Additionally, the shrine enshrines {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane|ja|天児屋根命|1=Ame-no-Koyane|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天児屋命}}, "Tsurugihiko no Kami" (剱彦神), {{ill|Oōusunomiko|ja|大碓皇子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oōusunomiko|1=Prince Oousu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11438297}}, "Kosunomiko" (小臼命, identified with Yamato Takeru), {{ill|Futodama|ja|太玉命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futodama|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to フトダマ}}, and {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}}. These deities were enshrined through amalgamation in 1910 (Meiji 43).
== History == Originally, the shrine was located atop Tsurugi Rock (剱岩), referred to as "Biwanku" (琵琶甲 or 枇琶甲), with a stone sanctuary at the summit and a worship hall at the base. In December 1674 (Enpō 1), it was relocated to its current site. The name "Tsurugi" derives from a legend where Yamato Takeru climbed the rock while scouting the [[:en:Kumaso|Kumaso]] during his conquest, resting with his sword.
During the reign of the Shikine clan, the shrine fell into neglect as they venerated [[:en:Tenmangū|Tenmangu]] as their ancestral shrine. It was later restored by [[:en:Shimazu Yoshihisa|Shimazu Yoshihisa]] under the order of {{ill|Yamada Toshiyasu|ja|山田利安|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamada Toshiyasu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Records of this restoration exist in the "Shrine Registry" and "Tsurugi Shrine History" preserved by the Saruwatari family of Shikine.
On October 25, 1910, the shrine amalgamated with Hyōsu Shrine, Sugawara Shrine, Futodama Shrine, and Hiruko Shrine.
== Festivals and Rituals == The annual "Shinkōsai" (神幸祭) or "Mikoshi Procession" originates from a historical event where a summer epidemic was halted after a purification ritual performed by the shrine. The festival is now held to pray for health and protection against epidemics.
Additionally, monthly rituals are conducted on the 1st and 15th at 6 AM.
== Amalgamated Shrines == ;Hyōsu Shrine : Deity and origin unknown. Previously located in Hamaji Setta, Shikine Village, with a tiled main hall and a dance hall.
;Sugawara Shrine : Dedicated to {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}}. Originally located in Setta, Shikine Village. Historical records mention its reconstruction in 1602 (Keichō 7) by Lord Yoshihisa Shimazu.
;Futodama Shrine : Dedicated to {{ill|Futodama|ja|太玉命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futodama|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to フトダマ}}. Details unknown. Originally had a tiled main hall, dance hall, and bridge hall.
;Hiruko Shrine : Dedicated to {{ill|Hiruko|ja|蛭子命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiruko|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヒルコ|1=Hiruko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1067028}}. Once featured a stone sanctuary in Setta, Shikine Village.

[[ja:剱神社]] <!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis--> {{Commons category|Tsurugi-jinja (Kirishima)}} {{wikidata link|Q11397991}} == Accidentally Overwritten Content == <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Tsurugi-jinja (Kirishima)]][[ja:剱神社]] {{Infobox religious building|image=File:Shikine-turugi.JPG|prefecture={{ill|Kokubu-Shikine|WD=Q11420538}}|coordinates={{Coord|31.70575|N|130.786528|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|architecture_style={{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|WD=Q930545}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11397991]] Wikidata name: Tsurugi-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:31, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:09, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Tsurugi Shrine''' (剱神社) often written as "剣神社," is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in {{ill|Kokubu Shikine|ja|国分敷根|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokubu Shikine|1=Kokubu-Shikine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11420538}}, [[:en:Kirishima, Kagoshima|Kirishima, Kagoshima]], [[:en:Kagoshima Prefecture|Kagoshima Prefecture]].
== Deities Enshrined == The principal deity is [[:en:Yamato Takeru|Yamato Takeru]]. Additionally, the shrine enshrines {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane|ja|天児屋根命|1=Ame-no-Koyane|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天児屋命}}, "Tsurugihiko no Kami" (剱彦神), {{ill|Oōusunomiko|ja|大碓皇子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oōusunomiko|1=Prince Oousu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11438297}}, "Kosunomiko" (小臼命, identified with Yamato Takeru), {{ill|Futodama|ja|太玉命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futodama|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to フトダマ}}, and {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}}. These deities were enshrined through amalgamation in 1910 (Meiji 43).
== History == Originally, the shrine was located atop Tsurugi Rock (剱岩), referred to as "Biwanku" (琵琶甲 or 枇琶甲), with a stone sanctuary at the summit and a worship hall at the base. In December 1674 (Enpō 1), it was relocated to its current site. The name "Tsurugi" derives from a legend where Yamato Takeru climbed the rock while scouting the [[:en:Kumaso|Kumaso]] during his conquest, resting with his sword.
During the reign of the Shikine clan, the shrine fell into neglect as they venerated [[:en:Tenmangū|Tenmangu]] as their ancestral shrine. It was later restored by [[:en:Shimazu Yoshihisa|Shimazu Yoshihisa]] under the order of {{ill|Yamada Toshiyasu|ja|山田利安|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamada Toshiyasu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Records of this restoration exist in the "Shrine Registry" and "Tsurugi Shrine History" preserved by the Saruwatari family of Shikine.
On October 25, 1910, the shrine amalgamated with Hyōsu Shrine, Sugawara Shrine, Futodama Shrine, and Hiruko Shrine.
== Festivals and Rituals == The annual "Shinkōsai" (神幸祭) or "Mikoshi Procession" originates from a historical event where a summer epidemic was halted after a purification ritual performed by the shrine. The festival is now held to pray for health and protection against epidemics.
Additionally, monthly rituals are conducted on the 1st and 15th at 6 AM.
== Amalgamated Shrines == ;Hyōsu Shrine : Deity and origin unknown. Previously located in Hamaji Setta, Shikine Village, with a tiled main hall and a dance hall.
;Sugawara Shrine : Dedicated to {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}}. Originally located in Setta, Shikine Village. Historical records mention its reconstruction in 1602 (Keichō 7) by Lord Yoshihisa Shimazu.
;Futodama Shrine : Dedicated to {{ill|Futodama|ja|太玉命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futodama|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to フトダマ}}. Details unknown. Originally had a tiled main hall, dance hall, and bridge hall.
;Hiruko Shrine : Dedicated to {{ill|Hiruko|ja|蛭子命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiruko|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヒルコ|1=Hiruko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1067028}}. Once featured a stone sanctuary in Setta, Shikine Village.
== Gallery == * Tsurugi Rock within the shrine precincts * Site of Futodama Shrine, now Shikine Community Hall * Site of Hiruko Shrine with a modern concrete sanctuary * Statue of Ebisu at the former site of Hiruko Shrine
== References == * "Furusato Shikine no Kamigami" edited by Tsurugi Shrine Chief Priest Ikeda Shin
== External Links == * [http://www.kagojinjacho.or.jp/search/airaisa/kirishima/post-687.html Tsurugi Shrine (Kagoshima Shrine Association)]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Tsurugi-jinja (Kirishima)]][[ja:剱神社]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Tsurugi-jinja (Kirishima)]][[ja:剱神社]] '''Tsurugi Shrine''' (剱神社) often written as "剣神社," is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} located in {{ill|Kokubu Shikine|ja|国分敷根|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Kirishima, Kagoshima|ja|霧島市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Kagoshima Prefecture|ja|鹿児島県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.
== Deities Enshrined == The principal deity is {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武命|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Additionally, the shrine enshrines {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane|ja|天児屋根命|1=Ame-no-Koyane|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, "Tsurugihiko no Kami" (剱彦神), {{ill|Oōusunomiko|ja|大碓皇子|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, "Kosunomiko" (小臼命, identified with Yamato Takeru), {{ill|Futodama|ja|太玉命|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. These deities were enshrined through amalgamation in 1910 (Meiji 43).
== History == Originally, the shrine was located atop Tsurugi Rock (剱岩), referred to as "Biwanku" (琵琶甲 or 枇琶甲), with a stone sanctuary at the summit and a worship hall at the base. In December 1674 (Enpō 1), it was relocated to its current site. The name "Tsurugi" derives from a legend where Yamato Takeru climbed the rock while scouting the {{ill|Kumaso|ja|熊襲|qq=draft|qq=draft}} during his conquest, resting with his sword.
During the reign of the Shikine clan, the shrine fell into neglect as they venerated {{ill|Tenmangu|ja|天満宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}} as their ancestral shrine. It was later restored by {{ill|Shimazu Yoshihisa|ja|島津義久|qq=draft|qq=draft}} under the order of {{ill|Yamada Toshiyasu|ja|山田利安|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Records of this restoration exist in the "Shrine Registry" and "Tsurugi Shrine History" preserved by the Saruwatari family of Shikine.
On October 25, 1910, the shrine amalgamated with Hyōsu Shrine, Sugawara Shrine, Futodama Shrine, and Hiruko Shrine.
== Festivals and Rituals == The annual "Shinkōsai" (神幸祭) or "Mikoshi Procession" originates from a historical event where a summer epidemic was halted after a purification ritual performed by the shrine. The festival is now held to pray for health and protection against epidemics.
Additionally, monthly rituals are conducted on the 1st and 15th at 6 AM.
== Amalgamated Shrines == ;Hyōsu Shrine : Deity and origin unknown. Previously located in Hamaji Setta, Shikine Village, with a tiled main hall and a dance hall.
;Sugawara Shrine : Dedicated to {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Originally located in Setta, Shikine Village. Historical records mention its reconstruction in 1602 (Keichō 7) by Lord Yoshihisa Shimazu.
;Futodama Shrine : Dedicated to {{ill|Futodama|ja|太玉命|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Details unknown. Originally had a tiled main hall, dance hall, and bridge hall.
;Hiruko Shrine : Dedicated to {{ill|Hiruko|ja|蛭子命|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Once featured a stone sanctuary in Setta, Shikine Village.
== Gallery == * Tsurugi Rock within the shrine precincts * Site of Futodama Shrine, now Shikine Community Hall * Site of Hiruko Shrine with a modern concrete sanctuary * Statue of Ebisu at the former site of Hiruko Shrine
== References == * "Furusato Shikine no Kamigami" edited by Tsurugi Shrine Chief Priest Ikeda Shin

== History == Originally, the shrine was located atop Tsurugi Rock (剱岩), referred to as "Biwanku" (琵琶甲 or 枇琶甲), with a stone sanctuary at the summit and a worship hall at the base. In December 1674 (Enpō 1), it was relocated to its current site. The name "Tsurugi" derives from a legend where Yamato Takeru climbed the rock while scouting the {{ill|Kumaso|ja|熊襲|qq=draft|qq=draft}} during his conquest, resting with his sword.
During the reign of the Shikine clan, the shrine fell into neglect as they venerated {{ill|Tenmangu|ja|天満宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}} as their ancestral shrine. It was later restored by {{ill|Shimazu Yoshihisa|ja|島津義久|qq=draft|qq=draft}} under the order of {{ill|Yamada Toshiyasu|ja|山田利安|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Records of this restoration exist in the "Shrine Registry" and "Tsurugi Shrine History" preserved by the Saruwatari family of Shikine.
On October 25, 1910, the shrine amalgamated with Hyōsu Shrine, Sugawara Shrine, Futodama Shrine, and Hiruko Shrine.
== Festivals and Rituals == The annual "Shinkōsai" (神幸祭) or "Mikoshi Procession" originates from a historical event where a summer epidemic was halted after a purification ritual performed by the shrine. The festival is now held to pray for health and protection against epidemics.
Additionally, monthly rituals are conducted on the 1st and 15th at 6 AM.
== Amalgamated Shrines == ;Hyōsu Shrine : Deity and origin unknown. Previously located in Hamaji Setta, Shikine Village, with a tiled main hall and a dance hall.
;Sugawara Shrine : Dedicated to {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Originally located in Setta, Shikine Village. Historical records mention its reconstruction in 1602 (Keichō 7) by Lord Yoshihisa Shimazu.
;Futodama Shrine : Dedicated to {{ill|Futodama|ja|太玉命|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Details unknown. Originally had a tiled main hall, dance hall, and bridge hall.
;Hiruko Shrine : Dedicated to {{ill|Hiruko|ja|蛭子命|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Once featured a stone sanctuary in Setta, Shikine Village.

Originally located in Setta, Shikine Village. Historical records mention its reconstruction in 1602 (Keichō 7) by Lord Yoshihisa Shimazu.
;Futodama Shrine : Dedicated to {{ill|Futodama|ja|太玉命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futodama|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to フトダマ}}. Details unknown. Originally had a tiled main hall, dance hall, and bridge hall.
;Hiruko Shrine : Dedicated to {{ill|Hiruko|ja|蛭子命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiruko|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヒルコ|1=Hiruko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1067028}}. Once featured a stone sanctuary in Setta, Shikine Village.
== Gallery == * Tsurugi Rock within the shrine precincts * Site of Futodama Shrine, now Shikine Community Hall * Site of Hiruko Shrine with a modern concrete sanctuary * Statue of Ebisu at the former site of Hiruko Shrine

{{translated page|ja|剱神社|version=91787801|comment=Imported full ja history}}
[[ja:剱神社]] <!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis--> {{Commons category|Tsurugi-jinja (Kirishima)}} {{wikidata link|Q11397991}} == Accidentally Overwritten Content == <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Tsurugi-jinja (Kirishima)]][[ja:剱神社]] {{Infobox religious building|image=File:Shikine-turugi.JPG|prefecture={{ill|Kokubu-Shikine|WD=Q11420538}}|coordinates={{Coord|31.70575|N|130.786528|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|architecture_style={{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|WD=Q930545}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11397991]] Wikidata name: Tsurugi-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:31, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:09, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Tsurugi Shrine''' (剱神社) often written as "剣神社," is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Kokubu Shikine|ja|国分敷根|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokubu Shikine|1=Kokubu-Shikine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11420538}}, {{ill|Kirishima, Kagoshima|en|Kirishima, Kagoshima|WD=Q858352|lt=Kirishima, Kagoshima}}, {{ill|Kagoshima Prefecture|en|Kagoshima Prefecture|WD=Q15701|lt=Kagoshima Prefecture}}.
== Deities Enshrined == The principal deity is {{ill|Yamato Takeru|en|Yamato Takeru|WD=Q461258|lt=Yamato Takeru}}. Additionally, the shrine enshrines {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane|ja|天児屋根命|1=Ame-no-Koyane|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天児屋命}}, "Tsurugihiko no Kami" (剱彦神), {{ill|Oōusunomiko|ja|大碓皇子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oōusunomiko|1=Prince Oousu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11438297}}, "Kosunomiko" (小臼命, identified with Yamato Takeru), {{ill|Futodama|ja|太玉命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futodama|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to フトダマ}}, and {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}}. These deities were enshrined through amalgamation in 1910 (Meiji 43).
== History == Originally, the shrine was located atop Tsurugi Rock (剱岩), referred to as "Biwanku" (琵琶甲 or 枇琶甲), with a stone sanctuary at the summit and a worship hall at the base. In December 1674 (Enpō 1), it was relocated to its current site. The name "Tsurugi" derives from a legend where Yamato Takeru climbed the rock while scouting the {{ill|Kumaso|en|Kumaso|WD=Q1196897|lt=Kumaso}} during his conquest, resting with his sword.
During the reign of the Shikine clan, the shrine fell into neglect as they venerated {{ill|Tenmangū|en|Tenmangū|WD=Q798838|lt=Tenmangu}} as their ancestral shrine. It was later restored by {{ill|Shimazu Yoshihisa|en|Shimazu Yoshihisa|WD=Q955727|lt=Shimazu Yoshihisa}} under the order of {{ill|Yamada Toshiyasu|ja|山田利安|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamada Toshiyasu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Records of this restoration exist in the "Shrine Registry" and "Tsurugi Shrine History" preserved by the Saruwatari family of Shikine.
On October 25, 1910, the shrine amalgamated with Hyōsu Shrine, Sugawara Shrine, Futodama Shrine, and Hiruko Shrine.
== Festivals and Rituals == The annual "Shinkōsai" (神幸祭) or "Mikoshi Procession" originates from a historical event where a summer epidemic was halted after a purification ritual performed by the shrine. The festival is now held to pray for health and protection against epidemics.
Additionally, monthly rituals are conducted on the 1st and 15th at 6 AM.
== Amalgamated Shrines == ;Hyōsu Shrine : Deity and origin unknown. Previously located in Hamaji Setta, Shikine Village, with a tiled main hall and a dance hall.
;Sugawara Shrine : Dedicated to {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}}. Originally located in Setta, Shikine Village. Historical records mention its reconstruction in 1602 (Keichō 7) by Lord Yoshihisa Shimazu.
;Futodama Shrine : Dedicated to {{ill|Futodama|ja|太玉命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futodama|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to フトダマ}}. Details unknown. Originally had a tiled main hall, dance hall, and bridge hall.
;Hiruko Shrine : Dedicated to {{ill|Hiruko|ja|蛭子命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiruko|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヒルコ|1=Hiruko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1067028}}. Once featured a stone sanctuary in Setta, Shikine Village.
== Gallery == * Tsurugi Rock within the shrine precincts * Site of Futodama Shrine, now Shikine Community Hall * Site of Hiruko Shrine with a modern concrete sanctuary * Statue of Ebisu at the former site of Hiruko Shrine
== References == * "Furusato Shikine no Kamigami" edited by Tsurugi Shrine Chief Priest Ikeda Shin
== External Links == * [http://www.kagojinjacho.or.jp/search/airaisa/kirishima/post-687.html Tsurugi Shrine (Kagoshima Shrine Association)]

[[co:commons:Category:Tsurugi-jinja (Kirishima)]][[ja:剱神社]] [[co:commons:Category:Tsurugi-jinja (Kirishima)]][[ja:剱神社]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Tsurugi Shrine (Kirishima)}} [[sn:Tsurugi Shrine (Kirishima)]] [[ja:%E5%89%B1%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]] [[en:Tsurugi Shrine (Kirishima)]] [[ja:剱神社]] [[sn:commons:Category:Tsurugi-jinja (Kirishima)]] [[ja:剱神社]] [[ja:剱神社]]<!--interwikis from wikidata--> [[sn:commons:Category:Tsurugi-jinja (Kirishima)]][[ja:剱神社]] {{Infobox religious building|image=File:Shikine-turugi.JPG|prefecture={{ill|Kokubu-Shikine|WD=Q11420538}}|coordinates={{Coord|31.70575|N|130.786528|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|architecture_style={{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|WD=Q930545}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11397991]] Wikidata name: Tsurugi-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:31, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:09, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Tsurugi Shrine''' (剱神社) often written as "剣神社," is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Kokubu Shikine|ja|国分敷根|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokubu Shikine|1=Kokubu-Shikine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11420538}}, {{ill|Kirishima, Kagoshima|en|Kirishima, Kagoshima|WD=Q858352|lt=Kirishima, Kagoshima}}, {{ill|Kagoshima Prefecture|en|Kagoshima Prefecture|WD=Q15701|lt=Kagoshima Prefecture}}.
== Deities Enshrined == The principal deity is {{ill|Yamato Takeru|en|Yamato Takeru|WD=Q461258|lt=Yamato Takeru}}. Additionally, the shrine enshrines {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane|ja|天児屋根命|1=Ame-no-Koyane|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天児屋命}}, "Tsurugihiko no Kami" (剱彦神), {{ill|Oōusunomiko|ja|大碓皇子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oōusunomiko|1=Prince Oousu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11438297}}, "Kosunomiko" (小臼命, identified with Yamato Takeru), {{ill|Futodama|ja|太玉命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futodama|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to フトダマ}}, and {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}}. These deities were enshrined through amalgamation in 1910 (Meiji 43).
== History == Originally, the shrine was located atop Tsurugi Rock (剱岩), referred to as "Biwanku" (琵琶甲 or 枇琶甲), with a stone sanctuary at the summit and a worship hall at the base. In December 1674 (Enpō 1), it was relocated to its current site. The name "Tsurugi" derives from a legend where Yamato Takeru climbed the rock while scouting the {{ill|Kumaso|en|Kumaso|WD=Q1196897|lt=Kumaso}} during his conquest, resting with his sword.
During the reign of the Shikine clan, the shrine fell into neglect as they venerated {{ill|Tenmangū|en|Tenmangū|WD=Q798838|lt=Tenmangu}} as their ancestral shrine. It was later restored by {{ill|Shimazu Yoshihisa|en|Shimazu Yoshihisa|WD=Q955727|lt=Shimazu Yoshihisa}} under the order of {{ill|Yamada Toshiyasu|ja|山田利安|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamada Toshiyasu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Records of this restoration exist in the "Shrine Registry" and "Tsurugi Shrine History" preserved by the Saruwatari family of Shikine.
On October 25, 1910, the shrine amalgamated with Hyōsu Shrine, Sugawara Shrine, Futodama Shrine, and Hiruko Shrine.
== Festivals and Rituals == The annual "Shinkōsai" (神幸祭) or "Mikoshi Procession" originates from a historical event where a summer epidemic was halted after a purification ritual performed by the shrine. The festival is now held to pray for health and protection against epidemics.
Additionally, monthly rituals are conducted on the 1st and 15th at 6 AM.
== Amalgamated Shrines == ;Hyōsu Shrine : Deity and origin unknown. Previously located in Hamaji Setta, Shikine Village, with a tiled main hall and a dance hall.
;Sugawara Shrine : Dedicated to {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}}. Originally located in Setta, Shikine Village. Historical records mention its reconstruction in 1602 (Keichō 7) by Lord Yoshihisa Shimazu.
;Futodama Shrine : Dedicated to {{ill|Futodama|ja|太玉命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futodama|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to フトダマ}}. Details unknown. Originally had a tiled main hall, dance hall, and bridge hall.
;Hiruko Shrine : Dedicated to {{ill|Hiruko|ja|蛭子命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiruko|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヒルコ|1=Hiruko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1067028}}. Once featured a stone sanctuary in Setta, Shikine Village.
== Gallery == * Tsurugi Rock within the shrine precincts * Site of Futodama Shrine, now Shikine Community Hall * Site of Hiruko Shrine with a modern concrete sanctuary * Statue of Ebisu at the former site of Hiruko Shrine
== References == * "Furusato Shikine no Kamigami" edited by Tsurugi Shrine Chief Priest Ikeda Shin
== External Links == * [http://www.kagojinjacho.or.jp/search/airaisa/kirishima/post-687.html Tsurugi Shrine (Kagoshima Shrine Association)]

== Deities Enshrined == The principal deity is {{ill|Yamato Takeru|en|Yamato Takeru|WD=Q461258|lt=Yamato Takeru}}. Additionally, the shrine enshrines {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane|ja|天児屋根命|1=Ame-no-Koyane|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天児屋命}}, "Tsurugihiko no Kami" (剱彦神), {{ill|Oōusunomiko|ja|大碓皇子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oōusunomiko|1=Prince Oousu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11438297}}, "Kosunomiko" (小臼命, identified with Yamato Takeru), {{ill|Futodama|ja|太玉命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futodama|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to フトダマ}}, and {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}}. These deities were enshrined through amalgamation in 1910 (Meiji 43).
== History == Originally, the shrine was located atop Tsurugi Rock (剱岩), referred to as "Biwanku" (琵琶甲 or 枇琶甲), with a stone sanctuary at the summit and a worship hall at the base. In December 1674 (Enpō 1), it was relocated to its current site. The name "Tsurugi" derives from a legend where Yamato Takeru climbed the rock while scouting the {{ill|Kumaso|en|Kumaso|WD=Q1196897|lt=Kumaso}} during his conquest, resting with his sword.
During the reign of the Shikine clan, the shrine fell into neglect as they venerated {{ill|Tenmangū|en|Tenmangū|WD=Q798838|lt=Tenmangu}} as their ancestral shrine. It was later restored by {{ill|Shimazu Yoshihisa|en|Shimazu Yoshihisa|WD=Q955727|lt=Shimazu Yoshihisa}} under the order of {{ill|Yamada Toshiyasu|ja|山田利安|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamada Toshiyasu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Records of this restoration exist in the "Shrine Registry" and "Tsurugi Shrine History" preserved by the Saruwatari family of Shikine.
On October 25, 1910, the shrine amalgamated with Hyōsu Shrine, Sugawara Shrine, Futodama Shrine, and Hiruko Shrine.
== Festivals and Rituals == The annual "Shinkōsai" (神幸祭) or "Mikoshi Procession" originates from a historical event where a summer epidemic was halted after a purification ritual performed by the shrine. The festival is now held to pray for health and protection against epidemics.
Additionally, monthly rituals are conducted on the 1st and 15th at 6 AM.
== Amalgamated Shrines == ;Hyōsu Shrine : Deity and origin unknown. Previously located in Hamaji Setta, Shikine Village, with a tiled main hall and a dance hall.
;Sugawara Shrine : Dedicated to {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}}. Originally located in Setta, Shikine Village. Historical records mention its reconstruction in 1602 (Keichō 7) by Lord Yoshihisa Shimazu.
;Futodama Shrine : Dedicated to {{ill|Futodama|ja|太玉命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futodama|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to フトダマ}}. Details unknown. Originally had a tiled main hall, dance hall, and bridge hall.
;Hiruko Shrine : Dedicated to {{ill|Hiruko|ja|蛭子命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiruko|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヒルコ|1=Hiruko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1067028}}. Once featured a stone sanctuary in Setta, Shikine Village.
== Gallery == * Tsurugi Rock within the shrine precincts * Site of Futodama Shrine, now Shikine Community Hall * Site of Hiruko Shrine with a modern concrete sanctuary * Statue of Ebisu at the former site of Hiruko Shrine
== References == * "Furusato Shikine no Kamigami" edited by Tsurugi Shrine Chief Priest Ikeda Shin
== External Links == * [http://www.kagojinjacho.or.jp/search/airaisa/kirishima/post-687.html Tsurugi Shrine (Kagoshima Shrine Association)]

Originally located in Setta, Shikine Village. Historical records mention its reconstruction in 1602 (Keichō 7) by Lord Yoshihisa Shimazu.
;Futodama Shrine : Dedicated to {{ill|Futodama|ja|太玉命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futodama|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to フトダマ}}. Details unknown. Originally had a tiled main hall, dance hall, and bridge hall.
;Hiruko Shrine : Dedicated to {{ill|Hiruko|ja|蛭子命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiruko|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヒルコ|1=Hiruko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1067028}}. Once featured a stone sanctuary in Setta, Shikine Village.

[[co:commons:Category:Tsurugi-jinja (Kirishima)]][[ja:剱神社]] [[co:commons:Category:Tsurugi-jinja (Kirishima)]][[ja:剱神社]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Tsurugi Shrine (Kirishima)}} [[sn:Tsurugi Shrine (Kirishima)]] [[ja:%E5%89%B1%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]] [[en:Tsurugi Shrine (Kirishima)]] [[ja:剱神社]] [[sn:commons:Category:Tsurugi-jinja (Kirishima)]] [[ja:剱神社]] [[ja:剱神社]]<!--interwikis from wikidata--> [[sn:commons:Category:Tsurugi-jinja (Kirishima)]][[ja:剱神社]] {{Infobox religious building|image=File:Shikine-turugi.JPG|prefecture={{ill|Kokubu-Shikine|WD=Q11420538|1=Kokubu-Shikine}}|coordinates={{Coord|31.70575|N|130.786528|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|architecture_style={{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|WD=Q930545|1=Shinmei-zukuri}}}}
'''Tsurugi Shrine''' (剱神社) often written as "剣神社," is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Kokubu Shikine|ja|国分敷根|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokubu Shikine|1=Kokubu-Shikine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11420538}}, {{ill|Kirishima, Kagoshima|en|Kirishima, Kagoshima|WD=Q858352|lt=Kirishima, Kagoshima|1=Kirishima, Kagoshima}}, {{ill|Kagoshima Prefecture|en|Kagoshima Prefecture|WD=Q15701|lt=Kagoshima Prefecture|1=Kagoshima Prefecture}}.
== Deities Enshrined == The principal deity is {{ill|Yamato Takeru|en|Yamato Takeru|WD=Q461258|lt=Yamato Takeru|1=Yamato Takeru}}. Additionally, the shrine enshrines {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane|ja|天児屋根命|1=Ame-no-Koyane|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天児屋命}}, "Tsurugihiko no Kami" (剱彦神), {{ill|Oōusunomiko|ja|大碓皇子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oōusunomiko|1=Ōusu-no-Mikoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11438297}}, "Kosunomiko" (小臼命, identified with Yamato Takeru), {{ill|Futodama|ja|太玉命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futodama|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to フトダマ}}, and {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}}. These deities were enshrined through amalgamation in 1910 (Meiji 43).
== History == Originally, the shrine was located atop Tsurugi Rock (剱岩), referred to as "Biwanku" (琵琶甲 or 枇琶甲), with a stone sanctuary at the summit and a worship hall at the base. In December 1674 (Enpō 1), it was relocated to its current site. The name "Tsurugi" derives from a legend where Yamato Takeru climbed the rock while scouting the {{ill|Kumaso|en|Kumaso|WD=Q1196897|lt=Kumaso|1=Kumaso}} during his conquest, resting with his sword.
During the reign of the Shikine clan, the shrine fell into neglect as they venerated {{ill|Tenmangū|en|Tenmangū|WD=Q798838|lt=Tenmangu|1=Tenmangū}} as their ancestral shrine. It was later restored by {{ill|Shimazu Yoshihisa|en|Shimazu Yoshihisa|WD=Q955727|lt=Shimazu Yoshihisa|1=Shimazu Yoshihisa}} under the order of {{ill|Yamada Toshiyasu|ja|山田利安|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamada Toshiyasu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Records of this restoration exist in the "Shrine Registry" and "Tsurugi Shrine History" preserved by the Saruwatari family of Shikine.
On October 25, 1910, the shrine amalgamated with Hyōsu Shrine, Sugawara Shrine, Futodama Shrine, and Hiruko Shrine.
== Festivals and Rituals == The annual "Shinkōsai" (神幸祭) or "Mikoshi Procession" originates from a historical event where a summer epidemic was halted after a purification ritual performed by the shrine. The festival is now held to pray for health and protection against epidemics.
Additionally, monthly rituals are conducted on the 1st and 15th at 6 AM.
== Amalgamated Shrines == ;Hyōsu Shrine : Deity and origin unknown. Previously located in Hamaji Setta, Shikine Village, with a tiled main hall and a dance hall.
;Sugawara Shrine : Dedicated to {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}}. Originally located in Setta, Shikine Village. Historical records mention its reconstruction in 1602 (Keichō 7) by Lord Yoshihisa Shimazu.
;Futodama Shrine : Dedicated to {{ill|Futodama|ja|太玉命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futodama|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to フトダマ}}. Details unknown. Originally had a tiled main hall, dance hall, and bridge hall.
;Hiruko Shrine : Dedicated to {{ill|Hiruko|ja|蛭子命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiruko|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヒルコ|1=Hiruko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1067028}}. Once featured a stone sanctuary in Setta, Shikine Village.
== Gallery == * Tsurugi Rock within the shrine precincts * Site of Futodama Shrine, now Shikine Community Hall * Site of Hiruko Shrine with a modern concrete sanctuary * Statue of Ebisu at the former site of Hiruko Shrine
== References == * "Furusato Shikine no Kamigami" edited by Tsurugi Shrine Chief Priest Ikeda Shin
== External Links == * [http://www.kagojinjacho.or.jp/search/airaisa/kirishima/post-687.html Tsurugi Shrine (Kagoshima Shrine Association)]

'''Tsurugi Shrine''' (剱神社) often written as "剣神社," is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Kokubu Shikine|ja|国分敷根|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokubu Shikine|1=Kokubu-Shikine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11420538}}, {{ill|Kirishima, Kagoshima|en|Kirishima, Kagoshima|WD=Q858352|lt=Kirishima, Kagoshima|1=Kirishima, Kagoshima}}, {{ill|Kagoshima Prefecture|en|Kagoshima Prefecture|WD=Q15701|lt=Kagoshima Prefecture|1=Kagoshima Prefecture}}.
== Deities Enshrined == The principal deity is {{ill|Yamato Takeru|en|Yamato Takeru|WD=Q461258|lt=Yamato Takeru|1=Yamato Takeru}}. Additionally, the shrine enshrines {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane|ja|天児屋根命|1=Ame-no-Koyane|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天児屋命}}, "Tsurugihiko no Kami" (剱彦神), {{ill|Oōusunomiko|ja|大碓皇子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oōusunomiko|1=Ōusu-no-Mikoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11438297}}, "Kosunomiko" (小臼命, identified with Yamato Takeru), {{ill|Futodama|ja|太玉命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futodama|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to フトダマ}}, and {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}}. These deities were enshrined through amalgamation in 1910 (Meiji 43).
== History == Originally, the shrine was located atop Tsurugi Rock (剱岩), referred to as "Biwanku" (琵琶甲 or 枇琶甲), with a stone sanctuary at the summit and a worship hall at the base. In December 1674 (Enpō 1), it was relocated to its current site. The name "Tsurugi" derives from a legend where Yamato Takeru climbed the rock while scouting the {{ill|Kumaso|en|Kumaso|WD=Q1196897|lt=Kumaso|1=Kumaso}} during his conquest, resting with his sword.
During the reign of the Shikine clan, the shrine fell into neglect as they venerated {{ill|Tenmangū|en|Tenmangū|WD=Q798838|lt=Tenmangu|1=Tenmangū}} as their ancestral shrine. It was later restored by {{ill|Shimazu Yoshihisa|en|Shimazu Yoshihisa|WD=Q955727|lt=Shimazu Yoshihisa|1=Shimazu Yoshihisa}} under the order of {{ill|Yamada Toshiyasu|ja|山田利安|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamada Toshiyasu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Records of this restoration exist in the "Shrine Registry" and "Tsurugi Shrine History" preserved by the Saruwatari family of Shikine.
On October 25, 1910, the shrine amalgamated with Hyōsu Shrine, Sugawara Shrine, Futodama Shrine, and Hiruko Shrine.
== Festivals and Rituals == The annual "Shinkōsai" (神幸祭) or "Mikoshi Procession" originates from a historical event where a summer epidemic was halted after a purification ritual performed by the shrine. The festival is now held to pray for health and protection against epidemics.
Additionally, monthly rituals are conducted on the 1st and 15th at 6 AM.
== Amalgamated Shrines == ;Hyōsu Shrine : Deity and origin unknown. Previously located in Hamaji Setta, Shikine Village, with a tiled main hall and a dance hall.
;Sugawara Shrine : Dedicated to {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}}. Originally located in Setta, Shikine Village. Historical records mention its reconstruction in 1602 (Keichō 7) by Lord Yoshihisa Shimazu.
;Futodama Shrine : Dedicated to {{ill|Futodama|ja|太玉命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futodama|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to フトダマ}}. Details unknown. Originally had a tiled main hall, dance hall, and bridge hall.
;Hiruko Shrine : Dedicated to {{ill|Hiruko|ja|蛭子命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiruko|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヒルコ|1=Hiruko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1067028}}. Once featured a stone sanctuary in Setta, Shikine Village.
== Gallery == * Tsurugi Rock within the shrine precincts * Site of Futodama Shrine, now Shikine Community Hall * Site of Hiruko Shrine with a modern concrete sanctuary * Statue of Ebisu at the former site of Hiruko Shrine
== References == * "Furusato Shikine no Kamigami" edited by Tsurugi Shrine Chief Priest Ikeda Shin
== External Links == * [http://www.kagojinjacho.or.jp/search/airaisa/kirishima/post-687.html Tsurugi Shrine (Kagoshima Shrine Association)]

== Deities Enshrined == The principal deity is {{ill|Yamato Takeru|en|Yamato Takeru|WD=Q461258|lt=Yamato Takeru|1=Yamato Takeru}}. Additionally, the shrine enshrines {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane|ja|天児屋根命|1=Ame-no-Koyane|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天児屋命}}, "Tsurugihiko no Kami" (剱彦神), {{ill|Oōusunomiko|ja|大碓皇子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oōusunomiko|1=Ōusu-no-Mikoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11438297}}, "Kosunomiko" (小臼命, identified with Yamato Takeru), {{ill|Futodama|ja|太玉命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futodama|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to フトダマ}}, and {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}}. These deities were enshrined through amalgamation in 1910 (Meiji 43).
== History == Originally, the shrine was located atop Tsurugi Rock (剱岩), referred to as "Biwanku" (琵琶甲 or 枇琶甲), with a stone sanctuary at the summit and a worship hall at the base. In December 1674 (Enpō 1), it was relocated to its current site. The name "Tsurugi" derives from a legend where Yamato Takeru climbed the rock while scouting the {{ill|Kumaso|en|Kumaso|WD=Q1196897|lt=Kumaso|1=Kumaso}} during his conquest, resting with his sword.
During the reign of the Shikine clan, the shrine fell into neglect as they venerated {{ill|Tenmangū|en|Tenmangū|WD=Q798838|lt=Tenmangu|1=Tenmangū}} as their ancestral shrine. It was later restored by {{ill|Shimazu Yoshihisa|en|Shimazu Yoshihisa|WD=Q955727|lt=Shimazu Yoshihisa|1=Shimazu Yoshihisa}} under the order of {{ill|Yamada Toshiyasu|ja|山田利安|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamada Toshiyasu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Records of this restoration exist in the "Shrine Registry" and "Tsurugi Shrine History" preserved by the Saruwatari family of Shikine.
On October 25, 1910, the shrine amalgamated with Hyōsu Shrine, Sugawara Shrine, Futodama Shrine, and Hiruko Shrine.
== Festivals and Rituals == The annual "Shinkōsai" (神幸祭) or "Mikoshi Procession" originates from a historical event where a summer epidemic was halted after a purification ritual performed by the shrine. The festival is now held to pray for health and protection against epidemics.
Additionally, monthly rituals are conducted on the 1st and 15th at 6 AM.
== Amalgamated Shrines == ;Hyōsu Shrine : Deity and origin unknown. Previously located in Hamaji Setta, Shikine Village, with a tiled main hall and a dance hall.
;Sugawara Shrine : Dedicated to {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}}. Originally located in Setta, Shikine Village. Historical records mention its reconstruction in 1602 (Keichō 7) by Lord Yoshihisa Shimazu.
;Futodama Shrine : Dedicated to {{ill|Futodama|ja|太玉命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futodama|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to フトダマ}}. Details unknown. Originally had a tiled main hall, dance hall, and bridge hall.
;Hiruko Shrine : Dedicated to {{ill|Hiruko|ja|蛭子命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiruko|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヒルコ|1=Hiruko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1067028}}. Once featured a stone sanctuary in Setta, Shikine Village.
== Gallery == * Tsurugi Rock within the shrine precincts * Site of Futodama Shrine, now Shikine Community Hall * Site of Hiruko Shrine with a modern concrete sanctuary * Statue of Ebisu at the former site of Hiruko Shrine
== References == * "Furusato Shikine no Kamigami" edited by Tsurugi Shrine Chief Priest Ikeda Shin
== External Links == * [http://www.kagojinjacho.or.jp/search/airaisa/kirishima/post-687.html Tsurugi Shrine (Kagoshima Shrine Association)]

;Sugawara Shrine : Dedicated to {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}}. Originally located in Setta, Shikine Village. Historical records mention its reconstruction in 1602 (Keichō 7) by Lord Yoshihisa Shimazu.
;Futodama Shrine : Dedicated to {{ill|Futodama|ja|太玉命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futodama|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to フトダマ}}. Details unknown. Originally had a tiled main hall, dance hall, and bridge hall.
;Hiruko Shrine : Dedicated to {{ill|Hiruko|ja|蛭子命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiruko|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヒルコ|1=Hiruko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1067028}}. Once featured a stone sanctuary in Setta, Shikine Village.
== Gallery == * Tsurugi Rock within the shrine precincts * Site of Futodama Shrine, now Shikine Community Hall * Site of Hiruko Shrine with a modern concrete sanctuary * Statue of Ebisu at the former site of Hiruko Shrine
== References == * "Furusato Shikine no Kamigami" edited by Tsurugi Shrine Chief Priest Ikeda Shin
